Quaggan is tired of destruction. Quaggan hears human cities have better life for quaggans. And more fish.
Quaggan wonders who swims faster, you or a rock. Quaggan believes it is the rock.
Quaggans are sea creatures that have been driven to shallow waters by predators. They're gentle and seek only a home where they can be at peace.

Quaggans are so grateful! Kidnapped quaggans are back home safe, quaggans' eggs are safe, and the krait have been taught a lesson! You a truly a great hero and a good friend.If you are ever in the area again, please come visit quaggans.—Leemoola
Quaggans have not had much good luck lately, but Reanna is special. She and others like you—quaggans love you. Coo!

Queen Jennah is a beloved by the humans of Kryta. Once you have her approval, you have the respect of the Krytan people.
Queen Jennah is in danger. You must take a stand, and stop Kellach before he can harm the queen. As General Almorra of the Vigil says, "Some must fight, so that all may be free."

Queen Jennah is in danger. You must take a stand, and stop Kellach before he can harm the queen. As it is written in the scrolls of the Priory, "Sometimes the fate of one individual can change the world."
Queen Jennah is in danger. You must take a stand, and stop Kellach before he can harm the queen. As the agents of the Order of Whispers say, 'What cannot be outfought must be outwitted.'

Queen Jennah is safe, and Kryta's Seraph can return to their struggles against the centaurs. But for you, a greater danger has been uncovered, and you know that your calling is to do more than stem the tide. You must fight the Elder Dragons—and somehow, find a way to win.

Queen Jennah was wise to anticipate that Ebonhawke would need assistance during this transitional period in our relationship with the charr. But I doubt even the queen could have guessed that this kind of aid would come from someone like you. Someone who, without living here, recognized what harm could come with neglecting such delicate social issues. I believe strongly that good works should be rewarded, and so I hope you accept this small compensation on behalf of the Fallen Angels.—Fallen Angel Makayla

Queen Jennah's watchknights are an outlier among semiautonomous constructs, designed by a single engineer with comparatively limited resources. While they are an extraordinary feat of mechanical and arcane artistry, they have limited noncombat utility.    A fractal exploring the impact on Kryta if Scarlet Briar had been allowed to collaborate with the crown engineer garnered some interest, but it was determined to be less valuable than monitoring the watchknights' ongoing development.

Queen's Heart Orphanage is run by devotees of Kormir and Dwayna. It provides a safe home and loving care to children who have been cast adrift through the ravages of war and strife. You can't fail them.
Queensdale Academy Wand Set now available!

Queensdale is the heartland of the reforged human nation of Kryta. These rolling hills and well-watered fields are threatened by marauding centaurs and thieving bandits. The Seraph serve as its protectors.
Queensdale: Don't let this disturb those who rest.

Questions continue to flood in on an almost hourly basis: Who—or what—is the Astral Ward?    Reports and rumors are growing by the day: the return of the mursaat and seers; people questioning if their neighbors have been secretly spying on them. Sightings of a so-called "flying city" over homes and villages. Apparent abductions of livestock and demands for recompense. Not all of these reports are valid, mind you.    How much information has gotten out is hard to judge, and we are still investigating possible sources for the leaks. While most questions are harmless, there is an undercurrent of wondering what, exactly, the Astral Ward has been doing for the last few years while Tyria was left to suffer. Should we continue to let things simmer? The charr is out of the bag, as they say.    While many of our alliance leaders have begun addressing such concerns within their individual communities, a larger announcement is in the works. This announcement will be used to formally introduce the Astral Ward to the rest of the world, as well as their cooperation with the Tyrian Alliance. Our hope is that people will see that organizations such as the ward will not go completely unsupervised.

Quiet Lagoon is having me look for his missing scouts. Riven Marl should be among them.

Quinn is your oldest friend. While he doesn't always make the best decisions, he's loyal. You've survived many scrapes and adventures together. He's probably hanging out in the old neighborhood, trying to charm a lady or talk some coins out of a passerby.

Quivering Warmth jammed the tip of her spear into the training dummy, almost yelling out in frustration in the process. It felt good to work out some of her anger. And she had a lot to be angry about, with her ex-boyfriend Devious Weasel trying to get her in trouble with the local Claw.    And then there was that strange newcomer, Rigid Branch... She gripped tighter onto the length of the spear shaft, thinking of those big paws of his. All of these other bearkin sent her into torpor, but that one...    A spear whipped past her, planting itself into the dummy's head and snapping her out of her thoughts. She looked back to see Rigid Branch approaching, holding another spear in his hand already. "Wow," she thought, staring at it. "That's the biggest spear I've ever seen. Is that why they call him Rigid Branch?"    Quivering Warmth remained frozen, overwhelmed by the moment, as Rigid Branch came over and pulled out the embedded spear. "Already in torpor, you are," he teased. She shook her head at him, flustered.    "You'll never get the job done, gripping it like that," he continued, his paws touching hers. He loosened her grip, transferring her paws over to the longer spear. He stood behind her, setting her feet, showing her how to position for maximum effectiveness.    As his big strong paws wrapped around hers, she couldn't help but wonder if there might be a different reason they called him Rigid Branch.
R'tchikk (Return to Secrets of the Obscure)

R'tchikk brought me tea while I sit and think. I am very tired of this war.    I think of Mabon's face, the single time he removed his mask... His eyes; pure, white flame. When I heard that he passed, that was all I could see.    It is not fear that I am feeling, but... I am sad. The world feels darker—colder without that flame.

Ragna teases me whenever we drink. "You do it so carefully, Wurmsbearer." She'd down a keg just to make a point, if she weren't carrying. A daughter, she thinks. Eir sent a number of herbal mixtures that aided her ahead of young Braham's birth, but Na misses ale.    Sejm and I are getting ready to travel far north to Saldistead once spring arrives; the storm is too brutal to cross now (and more importantly, I'd rather not encounter those beasts with cold feet). They've grown apart from us, our people. Knut is worried what they'll do in isolation.    For now, we fortify. Come winter, we'll have one more belly to keep full. A smaller one, but game is less bountiful on this side of the Shiverpeaks nonetheless. We all miss Hoelbrak.
